targetConcept: Ser vs Estar — the two Spanish 'to be' verbs (A1)
finalDecisionNote: FINAL — primary locked to 'Estoy Aquí' (Shakira) per team-lead 2026-05-18. Content-builder's draft is anchored to this song. M14 (Estar Deep Dive) currently has Estoy Aquí booked in modules.ts line 1133 — tracked in PENDING.md for swap when M14 is built. No further changes.

primarySong.title: Estoy Aquí
primarySong.conceptMatchRationale: Title IS the lesson — 'Estoy Aquí' uses estar twice: estar-for-state ('I am [longing/waiting]') AND estar-for-location ('here'). Verb density is exceptional — 'estoy' repeats throughout the chorus, giving the A1 listener the target form dozens of times in 4:10. Clean, family-friendly, no explicit content. Works for both pop and rnb genre slots per curriculum-architect's note.
primarySong.copyrightSafeNotes: Cite only individual verb forms ('estoy', 'soy', 'es', 'está', 'eres') and the 2-word title 'Estoy Aquí' (which IS the verb-form being taught — fair-use educational reference to a title is acceptable). NEVER reproduce verses, the chorus, or any full lyric line. Quiz items must test the Spanish ser/estar concept ('What does estoy mean?', 'When do you use estar?'), never lyric recall.
primarySong.verificationNotes: Spotify ID 4M1lEbqPzlEw1JYWB6aE7K confirmed as the canonical track from Pies Descalzos (1995). YouTube ID NmJHH026X0c is the official HD video on Shakira's verified channel (posted 2012-04-06, runtime 4:10). Duration 4:10 = 250s. Reine should still do a 1-click human spot-check on both links before shipping.
primarySong.downstreamConflictNote: modules.ts line 1133 currently lists 'Estoy Aquí' (Shakira) as the M14 (Estar — To Be Temporary) primary. M14 will now need a different estar-heavy track. Track in PENDING.md. Suggested replacements for M14: 'Calma' (Pedro Capó & Farruko) or 'Vivir Mi Vida' (Marc Anthony).

pop.title: Estoy Aquí
pop.notes: Same as primary — Shakira's Latin Pop is the ideal pop slot. Reuse the primary entry.
pop.copyrightSafeNotes: Same as primary — individual verb forms + 2-word title only.

reggaeton.title: Calma — Remix
reggaeton.notes: Family-friendly, ~2 billion YouTube views, slower tropical-reggaeton tempo (A1-accessible). Naturally uses 'estoy' in present-state descriptions and 'es' in trait descriptions. Pedro Capó is Puerto Rican (clear enunciation), Farruko verse is short and clean.
reggaeton.copyrightSafeNotes: Cite only individual ser/estar forms ('estoy', 'es') as concept evidence. Title 'Calma' (interjection/noun) is safe metadata. Do NOT reproduce chorus.

rnb.title: Estoy Aquí
rnb.notes: Per curriculum-architect's note ('works for pop AND rnb genre tracks'), Estoy Aquí covers the rnb slot via its soulful-ballad lane. ALTERNATE PROPOSAL still acceptable per team-lead: if Reine wants a distinct rnb track, swap to 'La Tortura' (Shakira ft. Alejandro Sanz) spotifyId 5BSclXJTa9B0iURhUjZo50 — verified, soulful-flamenco lane, contains both 'soy' and 'está' in the duet exchange. youtubeId for La Tortura would need spot-check (Dsp_8Lm1eSk located via search but not WebFetch-verified).
rnb.copyrightSafeNotes: Same as primary — individual verb forms + 2-word title only.

regional-mexican.title: El Rey
regional-mexican.notes: Canonical 'Yo soy el rey' ranchera — likely the single most recognizable ser-identity statement in regional Mexican music. Slow, declamatory mariachi delivery is ideal for A1 listeners parsing every syllable. Cultural cornerstone — gives the content-builder a strong reading-passage hook for Mexican ranchera tradition. Family-friendly, no slang, no explicit. 2:58 = 178s fits the Listen-screen timer.
regional-mexican.copyrightSafeNotes: Cite only individual ser forms ('soy', 'es') as concept evidence. 2-word title 'El Rey' is safe metadata. NEVER reproduce the chorus line 'Yo soy el rey' as a quiz prompt — instead, use 'soy' alone as the verb-form evidence and quiz the concept ('What does soy mean?').

rejectedCandidates.title: Vivir Mi Vida
rejectedCandidates.reason: Was briefly primary during back-and-forth on 2026-05-18; final decision per team-lead is 'Estoy Aquí' (Shakira). Vivir Mi Vida remains a strong M14 (Estar deep dive) candidate when M14 is built and needs a new primary.

rejectedCandidates.title: Soy Yo
rejectedCandidates.reason: Locked to M5 (subject pronouns primary) AND booked for Module 13 (Ser — To Be Permanent) in modules.ts line 1102. Cannot reuse in M6.

rejectedCandidates.title: Loco
rejectedCandidates.reason: Booked for Module 15 (Ser vs Estar Deep Dive). Save for the A2 deep-dive module where the meaning-shift adjective focus matters more.

rejectedCandidates.title: Eres Mi Religión
rejectedCandidates.reason: Strong concept match (title is 'You are my religion') BUT Maná is already used as M3 primary ('¿Dónde Jugarán los Niños?'). Avoid artist clustering in the first 6 modules.

rejectedCandidates.title: La Camisa Negra
rejectedCandidates.reason: Strong A1 candidate ('tengo la camisa negra' + 'estoy mintiendo') BUT Juanes is already M4 primary ('A Dios Le Pido'). Avoid back-to-back artist reuse.

rejectedCandidates.title: Como La Flor
rejectedCandidates.reason: Cumbia-Tejano classic but the concept evidence is weak for ser/estar — the song's grammar focus is more on 'cómo' (how) and past-tense framing. Save Selena for a later module focused on adjectives or past tense.

rejectedCandidates.title: Color Esperanza
rejectedCandidates.reason: Inspirational pop with some ser-themed identity language but the verb density on ser/estar specifically is lower than Estoy Aquí or Vivir Mi Vida.

rejectedCandidates.title: Soy
rejectedCandidates.reason: Curriculum-architect's regional-Mexican backup. Could not surface a verifiable Spotify/YouTube ID for a song titled exactly 'Soy' by Alejandro Fernández. Picked 'El Rey' (Vicente Fernández) for the regional-mexican slot instead.

rejectedCandidates.title: Tú Eres
rejectedCandidates.reason: Curriculum-architect's rnb/bachata backup. Could not surface a clean Spotify/YouTube ID for a Juan Luis Guerra track titled exactly 'Tú Eres' on the Bachata Rosa album.

rejectedCandidates.title: Cómo Estás
rejectedCandidates.reason: Curriculum-architect's reggaeton backup. Search returned Daddy Yankee's catalog but no track titled exactly 'Cómo Estás'. Picked 'Calma' Remix instead.

rejectedCandidates.title: Sigo Siendo el Rey
rejectedCandidates.reason: Curriculum-architect's regional-Mexican backup. 'Siendo' (gerund of ser) is a stretch for A1. Picked the original 'El Rey' instead — same artist, A1-clean present-tense 'soy'.

rejectedCandidates.title: Eres
rejectedCandidates.reason: Was briefly the pop alternative during back-and-forth. Excellent concept match (title is the ser conjugation 'you are') but the final pop slot reuses the primary 'Estoy Aquí' per team-lead's locked decision. Reserve for a later module focused on 2nd-person singular forms.

copyrightSafetyOverall: All distinct tracks (Estoy Aquí covering pop+rnb / Calma Remix / El Rey) are mainstream major-label releases with public Spotify + YouTube metadata. Concept evidence is strictly limited to individual ser/estar verb forms ('soy', 'eres', 'es', 'estoy', 'estás', 'está'). 2-word song titles ('Estoy Aquí', 'El Rey', 'Calma') are safe to display as metadata — 'Estoy Aquí' is itself the verb form being taught (fair-use educational title reference). Quiz items must test the Spanish ser/estar concept, never lyric recall. Reading-comprehension passages must be 100% original.
knownBugFlags: Module 3 quiz dead-end — flagged per role spec, not specific to M6.
'¿Dónde jugarán los niños?' wrong YouTube Music link — flagged per role spec, M6 does not use Maná so this bug does not apply here.
Timer notification issue in YouTube Music — flagged per role spec, not specific to M6.

rnb slot REUSES the primary (Estoy Aquí). If Reine wants distinct songs per genre, swap to 'La Tortura' (Shakira ft. Alejandro Sanz, 5BSclXJTa9B0iURhUjZo50) — alternate still acceptable per team-lead.
